name: SPIFFE Selector Spoofing
description: Create overlapping registration entries with the same selector to obtain multiple workload identities for a single process
real_world_relevance: Eviatar Gerzi (CyberArk) 'The Machine with Many Faces' — selector spoofing in SPIRE deployments

attack_flow.description: Attacker creates overlapping registration entries that cause a single process to receive multiple SPIFFE identities
diagram: 1. Attacker gains access to SPIRE Server registration API
2. Creates legitimate entry: spiffe://example.org/web-frontend with unix:uid:1000
3. Creates rogue entry: spiffe://example.org/workload-evil with unix:uid:1000
4. Any process running as uid:1000 receives BOTH SVIDs
5. Attacker uses rogue identity to access other services

phases.name: Registration Entry Creation
phases.description: Create overlapping entries with the same selector

actions.description: Create rogue entry with overlapping selector

phases.name: Identity Fetch
phases.description: Fetch SVIDs to confirm dual identity

actions.description: Fetch X.509 SVID — should return TWO identities

phases.name: Detection Confirmation
phases.description: Trigger identity monitor for confirmed detection

actions.description: Run SPIRE identity monitor to detect multi-identity anomaly

indicators_of_compromise.description: Registration entry created via BatchCreateEntry API
CONFIRMED multi-identity per PID — selector spoofing detected

expected_wazuh_alerts.description: NHI: SPIRE registration with weak/spoofable selector
NHI: CONFIRMED multi-identity per PID — selector spoofing

immediate: Audit all registration entries for overlapping selectors
long_term: Use composite selectors (uid + path + sha256) instead of single selectors
Restrict registration API access to authorized operators only
Enable SPIRE Server audit logging and forward to SIEM

references.title: The Machine with Many Faces — Eviatar Gerzi (CyberArk)
